claim: The SICF arbitrator output for P11 (cli_log/2026-05-05.md L580-636 + L660-689; SICF run sha256 b3b339507ec0337e7217d426cacf0c64a9c94c37192ff76b813f6a3b750b3453) enumerates exactly four realistic options under the heading 'Realistic options:' (L613): (1) No-op (wait for referee verdict), (2) Voluntary corrigendum, (3) Withdrawal + restructure, (4) Hold for JTNB-2400 leverage. Verbatim text reproduced in sicf_options_verbatim.md (sha256 0AF25F0C2BC4E308E054DFE182AF593E015E824DA2F8F8FE7DCF246BDEDDC2DD) section A.

Selected option for the relay-049 P11 SICF four-options decision (W20 re-fire 2026-05-06): Option 3 - Withdrawal + restructure (resubmit). Selection basis: FORCED_BY_ELIMINATION under post-2026-05-06 hard-reject reality; Options 1, 2, 4 dominated per sicf_options_scoring_matrix.json (sha256 1049A94840F1793F61C164A553D567423760AF58E83F672C21F579F5C5BAE1BC) decision_summary.selected_option_id = 3, selected_option_name = 'Withdrawal + restructure (resubmit)'. Operator-pasted JTNB-2400 verdict status RECEIVED_NEGATIVE (2026-05-06; rejection language verbatim: 'After consideration, we feel that the content of the manuscript does not meet the standards expected for publication in the journal') drives the elimination logic.

binding_window_days_remaining = 30 (operator-conceptual SICF-roadmap freshness window, NOT journal-imposed). Rationale: jtnb_policy_snapshot.md (sha256 7B41F5D318FC3B2A7F1B0AEB494D7117A0FA7E5C2F29462191097A051D15F513) section D records that JTNB instructions-to-authors page (https://jtnb.centre-mersenne.org/page/instructions-to-authors/) returned no published 'withdraw-and-resubmit window' policy via fetch_webpage 2026-05-06; the only published JTNB constraint with submission-window semantics is the originality clause ('publishes original papers ... not published elsewhere', jtnb.centre-mersenne.org root). Under the post-verdict hard reject, the relay-049 prompt's 'JTNB withdraw-and-resubmit window narrowing' framing reflects an operator-conceptual freshness window. The 30-day estimate is the conservative SICF-roadmap freshness window: 4 fatals enumerated 2026-05-05; restructure dispatch within 30 days keeps fatal-fix mental model intact and avoids fresh SICF re-run pre-resubmission. JUDGMENT CALL J3 in handoff.md.

manuscript_substance_preservation_pct = 80.0 for the selected Option 3. Per sicf_options_scoring_matrix.json options[2].scoring_post_verdict_actual_RECEIVED_NEGATIVE.axis_1_substance_pct = 80 with axis_1_note: 'fatals #1-#3 reframe to T1-relative + algorithm-conditional (no semantic loss in numerical content; loss is in the rhetorical strength of Theorem 4.1 completeness claim and Theorem 5.3 transcendence-proof status). Numerical classification table (513,387/400,093/24/etc.) preserved verbatim. Mobius identification (Prop 5.2) demoted from theorem-grade to conjecture-grade; Theorem 5.3 (transcendence of K) demoted to Conjecture 5.3* or removed from base case. ~80% of original manuscript's substance preserved; ~20% lost in named-theorem demotion + scope qualification.' Estimate basis is qualitative judgment grounded in the four-fatals + #18 inventory at sicf_options_verbatim.md section C; not a measured metric. JUDGMENT CALL J4 in handoff.md.

Relay 049 STEP 7 self-check HALT_049_DECISION_INCLUDES_FRAMING: PASS. Programmatic framing-word scan on sicf_decision.md (sha256 to be recorded in handoff.md final_anchor_hashes; pattern '(I believe|we argue|in my view|in our opinion|it seems|clearly|obviously|importantly|crucially|fundamentally|undoubtedly|certainly|definitely|surely|\bbelieve\b|\bargue\b|\bshows\b|\bconfirms\b|\bproves\b|\bdemonstrates\b|\bestablishes\b|should|must|ought)' via PowerShell Select-String, 0 hits in non-quoted prose. Cited blocks (verbatim quotes from cli_log/2026-05-05.md or jtnb.centre-mersenne.org) are exempt per substrate-only convention.

Relay 049 STEP 7 self-check HALT_049_GROUNDING_PARTIAL: PASS / not triggered. All five sources accessible; no source unreachable; classifier-vs-preprint stratum amendment not applicable (prose-decision task, not empirical-claim task).
